skill.name: awesome-repo-builder
skill.description: Create a topic-specific GitHub awesome-list repository scaffold with a polished README, concise contribution rules, AI-agent instructions, URL verification script, license, and reusable templates.
skill.summary: Generate complete awesome-list repository scaffolding from a JSON spec.
skill.author: zjsxply

security_audit.summary: All 109 static findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives. The external_commands detections were triggered by markdown backticks in documentation strings. The network detections were hardcoded URLs inside template strings for generated README badges and license text. The weak cryptographic algorithm and credential access findings had no basis in the actual code. The skill is a documentation generator that writes files to a user-specified output directory with no code execution, network requests, or credential access.

content.user_title: Build polished awesome-list repositories in seconds
content.value_statement: Creating a well-structured awesome-list repository requires repetitive boilerplate for README formatting, contribution rules, and agent instructions. This skill automates the entire scaffold generation from a simple JSON spec, producing a professional repository with badges, categorized entries, and reusable templates.
actual_capabilities: Generate a complete awesome-list repository scaffold from a JSON spec
Create polished README.md with centered header, badges, and table of contents
Produce CONTRIBUTING.md with inclusion criteria and exclusion rules
Generate AGENTS.md with repository conventions for AI agents
Write reusable Markdown templates for planning, implementation, and review
Include a standalone verify_urls.py script for checking link health
Support nested taxonomy with sections and subsections
limitations: Does not perform actual web research or validate URLs automatically
Requires a JSON spec as input; cannot infer topic structure from free text alone
Generated content is English-only; manual translation is required for other languages
URL verification script must be run manually after generation

use_cases.title: Bootstrap a new community resource list
use_cases.description: A developer wants to start an awesome-list for a niche topic. They provide a topic and taxonomy, and the skill generates the full repository structure ready for GitHub.

use_cases.title: Standardize internal knowledge bases
use_cases.description: A team lead creates curated internal resource lists with consistent formatting, contribution rules, and AI-agent instructions across multiple projects.

use_cases.title: Generate conference or event resource catalogs
use_cases.description: An event organizer builds a structured repository of talks, tools, and papers with reusable review checklists and planning templates.

prompt_templates.title: Generate an awesome repo for a topic
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an awesome-list repository for the topic: machine learning interpretability. Include sections for survey papers, open source tools, benchmarks, and educational resources. Generate the scaffold in ./awesome-ml-interpretability.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: single topic with default taxonomy

prompt_templates.title: Build a repo with custom criteria
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to create an awesome list for Rust game development. The inclusion criteria are: open source tools, actively maintained crates, and tutorials with working examples. Exclude commercial engines without source code. Generate the repository scaffold.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: topic with custom inclusion and exclusion rules

prompt_templates.title: Generate from a researched entry list
prompt_templates.prompt: I have already researched 20 entries for an awesome list on time-series databases. Here is my JSON spec with taxonomy and entries. Please generate the full repository scaffold and verify the output structure.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: user provides a complete spec with pre-researched entries

prompt_templates.title: Create a repo with templates and agent instructions
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an awesome-list repository for LLM evaluation frameworks. Include templates for PLAN.md, IMPLEMENT.md, and CHECKLIST.md. The AGENTS.md should instruct contributors to verify all URLs and keep entries concise. Output to ./awesome-llm-eval.
prompt_templates.scenario: Expert: full customization with templates and agent conventions

output_examples.input: Create an awesome list for future event prediction
output_examples.output: Generated awesome-future-event-prediction/ with README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, AGENTS.md, LICENSE, .gitignore, verify_urls.py, and templates/ folder containing PLAN.md, IMPLEMENT.md, CHECKLIST.md, and AGENTS.md.

output_examples.input: Build a repo for Rust CLI tools with custom criteria
output_examples.output: The skill creates a scaffold where CONTRIBUTING.md specifies that tools must be open source and cross-platform. AGENTS.md instructs AI contributors to sort entries by problem domain rather than author. The README includes a centered title, badges, and a table of contents linking to categorized sections.

best_practices: Provide a clear topic and at least a draft taxonomy for the best results
Review the generated AGENTS.md to ensure conventions match your project needs
Run the generated verify_urls.py after adding external links to catch broken URLs early
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to generate lists without any initial research or seed entries
Avoid adding complex governance, PR automation, or scheduled maintenance unless explicitly requested
Do not rely on the placeholder example.com links in empty sections; replace them with real resources

faq.question: What inputs does the skill need to generate a repository?
faq.answer: The minimum input is a topic. For better results, provide a taxonomy, inclusion criteria, and optional initial entries. The skill will infer defaults for missing fields.

faq.question: Does the skill verify that URLs are reachable?
faq.answer: The skill generates a verify_urls.py script in the output directory. You must run this script manually to check link health. The skill itself does not make network requests.

faq.question: Can I customize the generated templates?
faq.answer: Yes. The templates are standard Markdown files. You can edit them after generation, or modify the skill script to change default template content.

faq.question: What license does the generated repository use?
faq.answer: The default license is CC0 1.0 Universal. You can specify a different license by modifying the generated LICENSE file or adjusting the spec.

faq.question: Is the generated repository ready to publish on GitHub immediately?
faq.answer: The scaffold includes all standard files, but you should review the content, replace placeholder links, and verify URLs before publishing.

faq.question: Does this skill require any external dependencies?
faq.answer: No. The generation script uses only the Python standard library. The generated verify_urls.py also uses only standard library modules.
